name: Dudley Brooks
abstract: Dudley Brooks (December 22, 1913 – July 17, 1989) was an American jazz pianist, arranger, and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Red Callender, and myself, and Dudley Brooks, piano player, we were on staff at Paramount Studios.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Luther Henderson and Dudley Brooks were two of our chief arrangers on staff at Great Lakes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: What about the Brooks family, Dudley Brooks and that gang? [INTERVIEWEE] Dudley? Well, yeah, let me tell you about Dudley. Dudley, he took a little sick and wasnt very well, so Dudley quit playing, he quit playing.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: they had all these great musicians: Clark Terry and Osie Johnson and Dudley Brooks and all these famous musicians... I went into the Navy in 1942
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I'll tell you who made the arrangements for us -- Gerald Wilson was making arrangements for us, Dudley Brooks, and Billy Strayhorn.

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: And also you have people like Dudley Brooks, Eddie Beal. These people were all here when I first came here.
